lessons.name: Unit 2: City Life
lessons.description: Learn about city life: urban features, city problems, double comparatives, and phrasal verbs.

sub_lessons.name: Getting Started
sub_lessons.description: Khởi động - Làm quen với chủ đề City Life qua hội thoại và từ vựng.

sections.heading: 1. Listen and Read
sections.content:
Dialogue:

Tom: Hi, Phong. You look happy today.

Phong: Yes, my family moved to a new flat in the city centre last week.

Tom: Oh, really? How do you like city life?

Phong: I love it! It's much more exciting than living in the suburb. Everything is convenient. There are shopping malls, cinemas, and restaurants nearby.

Tom: What about the transport?

Phong: It's easy to get around. There are many buses and the metro is fast. The more the city develops, the better the public transport becomes.

Tom: Sounds great! But isn't it noisy and crowded?

Phong: Well, the bigger the city is, the more crowded it gets. But I don't mind. I enjoy the busy atmosphere.

Tom: I think I should visit your new place sometime.

Phong: Sure! I can show you around.

sections.heading: 2. Vocabulary from the dialogue
sections.content:
Word / PhraseMeaning
city centretrung tâm thành phố
convenientthuận tiện
get aroundđi lại, di chuyển
metrotàu điện ngầm
public transportphương tiện công cộng
atmospherebầu không khí
show arounddẫn đi tham quan

sections.heading: 3. THIS UNIT INCLUDES
sections.content:
• Vocabulary: City features, city problems, city advantages
  • Grammar: Double comparatives (So sánh kép), Phrasal verbs
  • Skills: Reading for specific information about city problems, Talking about city problems and their solutions, Listening about city life, Writing a paragraph about city life

exercises.question: Where did Phong's family move to?
exercises.explanation: Phong said: 'my family moved to a new flat in the city centre last week.'

exercises.question: How does Phong feel about city life?
exercises.explanation: Phong said: 'I love it! It's much more exciting than living in the suburb.'

exercises.question: What does Phong say about public transport?
exercises.explanation: Phong said: 'It's easy to get around. There are many buses and the metro is fast.'

exercises.question: The phrase 'get around' means _____.
exercises.explanation: get around = đi lại, di chuyển

exercises.question: What does Phong NOT mind about city life?
exercises.explanation: Phong said: 'the bigger the city is, the more crowded it gets. But I don't mind. I enjoy the busy atmosphere.'

sub_lessons.name: A Closer Look 1
sub_lessons.description: Nhìn sâu 1 - Từ vựng về city features, city problems, city advantages và phát âm.

sections.heading: 1. Vocabulary: City features
sections.content:
WordMeaning
skyscrapernhà chọc trời
shopping malltrung tâm thương mại
public transportphương tiện công cộng
metro / subwaytàu điện ngầm
entertainmentgiải trí
infrastructurecơ sở hạ tầng

sections.heading: 2. City Problems (Vấn đề đô thị)
sections.content:
ProblemMeaningExample
traffic jamkẹt xeThere are traffic jams every rush hour.
air pollutionô nhiễm không khíAir pollution affects our health.
noise pollutionô nhiễm tiếng ồnNoise pollution from traffic is terrible.
overcrowdingquá đông đúcOvercrowding is a serious problem.
high cost of livingchi phí sinh hoạt caoThe cost of living in the city is high.
crimetội phạmCrime rates are higher in big cities.

sections.heading: 3. City Advantages (Lợi ích thành phố)
sections.content:
AdvantageMeaningExample
convenientthuận tiệnEverything is convenient in the city.
modernhiện đạiThe city has modern facilities.
job opportunitiescơ hội việc làmThere are many job opportunities.
educationgiáo dụcThe city has good schools.
healthcarechăm sóc sức khỏeHealthcare services are excellent.
entertainmentgiải tríThere are cinemas and parks.

sections.heading: 4. Pronunciation: Word stress
sections.content:
Quy tắc: Các từ kết thúc bằng -ity, -ion, -ic, -ial, -ian thường có trọng âm rơi vào âm tiết liền trước hậu tố.

WordStress
facilityfaCILity
opportunityopporTUNity
entertainmententerTAINment
infrastructureINfrastructure
pollutionpolLUtion

exercises.question: A _____ is a very tall building in a city.
exercises.explanation: skyscraper = nhà chọc trời

exercises.question: _____ is the system of buses, trains, etc. that people use to travel.
exercises.explanation: public transport = phương tiện công cộng

exercises.question: What is a major problem in big cities?
exercises.explanation: Traffic jam (kẹt xe) is a major problem in big cities.

exercises.question: The word 'pollution' has stress on which syllable?
exercises.explanation: pollution → polLUtion, trọng âm rơi vào âm tiết thứ hai.

exercises.question: Which is an ADVANTAGE of living in a city?
exercises.explanation: Job opportunities (cơ hội việc làm) is an advantage of city life.

exercises.question: The word 'opportunity' has stress on which syllable?
exercises.explanation: opportunity → opporTUNity, trọng âm rơi vào âm tiết thứ ba.

sub_lessons.name: A Closer Look 2
sub_lessons.description: Nhìn sâu 2 - Ngữ pháp: Double Comparatives (so sánh kép) và Phrasal verbs.

sections.heading: 1. Double Comparatives (So sánh kép)
sections.content:
Cấu trúc chung: The + comparative + S + V, the + comparative + S + V

Nghĩa: Càng... càng...

Các dạng:

LoạiCấu trúcVí dụ
Tính từ ngắnThe + adj-er + S + V, the + adj-er + S + VThe bigger the city is, the more crowded it is.
Tính từ dàiThe more + adj + S + V, the more + adj + S + VThe more expensive the city is, the more difficult life is.
Kết hợpThe + adj-er + S + V, the more + adj + S + VThe older I get, the more experienced I become.
Với danh từThe more + N + S + V, the more + N + S + VThe more money you have, the more problems you face.

sections.heading: 2. Phrasal Verbs (Cụm động từ)
sections.content:
Phrasal VerbMeaningExample
get aroundđi lại, di chuyểnIt's easy to get around by metro.
grow uplớn lênI grew up in Ha Noi.
come backquay lạiShe will come back tomorrow.
go ontiếp tụcPlease go on with your work.
take offcất cánh (máy bay)The plane took off on time.
deal withgiải quyếtWe must deal with traffic jams.
run out ofhết, cạn kiệtWe ran out of time.
keep up withtheo kịpI can't keep up with changes.
put onmặc vàoPut on your coat.
take overtiếp quảnHe took over the business.

problemTypes.name: Double Comparatives - Tính từ ngắn
problemTypes.method:
Cấu trúc: The + adj-er + S + V, the + adj-er + S + V

Chú ý: Tính từ ngắn (1-2 âm tiết) thêm -er. Nhân đôi phụ âm cuối nếu cần (hot → hotter).

examples.question: The city is big. It is crowded. → The ______ the city is, the ______ it is.
examples.answer: The bigger the city is, the more crowded it is.

examples.question: You drive fast. It is dangerous. → The ______ you drive, the ______ it is.
examples.answer: The faster you drive, the more dangerous it is.

examples.question: The weather is hot. I feel tired. → The ______ the weather is, the ______ I feel.
examples.answer: The hotter the weather is, the more tired I feel.

problemTypes.name: Double Comparatives - Tính từ dài
problemTypes.method:
Cấu trúc: The more + adj + S + V, the more + adj + S + V

Chú ý: Tính từ dài (3+ âm tiết) dùng more + adj.

examples.question: The city is expensive. Life is difficult. → The ______ the city is, the ______ life is.
examples.answer: The more expensive the city is, the more difficult life is.
examples.explanation: expensive → more expensive; difficult → more difficult

examples.question: It is polluted. It is unhealthy. → The ______ it is, the ______ it is.
examples.answer: The more polluted it is, the more unhealthy it is.
examples.explanation: polluted → more polluted; unhealthy → more unhealthy

examples.question: You are careful. You make fewer mistakes. → The ______ you are, the ______ mistakes you make.
examples.answer: The more careful you are, the fewer mistakes you make.

problemTypes.name: Phrasal Verbs - City Life
problemTypes.method: Phương pháp: Học thuộc nghĩa phrasal verbs và chọn phù hợp ngữ cảnh.

examples.question: It's easy to ______ in this city. (đi lại)
examples.answer: It's easy to get around in this city.

examples.question: We need to ______ this problem. (giải quyết)
examples.answer: We need to deal with this problem.

exercises.question: The bigger the city is, the ______ it is.
exercises.explanation: crowded (dài) → more crowded

exercises.question: The ______ you drive, the more dangerous it is.
exercises.explanation: fast → faster

exercises.question: The more expensive the city is, the ______ life is.
exercises.explanation: difficult (dài) → more difficult

exercises.question: The ______ the weather is, the more tired I feel.
exercises.explanation: hot → hotter (nhân đôi phụ âm)

exercises.question: I ______ in Hanoi. (lớn lên)
exercises.explanation: grow up = lớn lên (quá khứ: grew up)

exercises.question: It's easy to ______ in this city. (đi lại)
exercises.explanation: get around = đi lại, di chuyển

exercises.question: We need to ______ this problem. (giải quyết)
exercises.explanation: deal with = giải quyết

exercises.question: The older I get, the ______ I become.
exercises.explanation: experienced (dài) → more experienced

exercises.question: We are ______ time. (hết thời gian)
exercises.explanation: run out of = hết, cạn kiệt

exercises.question: It's hard to ______ the changes. (theo kịp)
exercises.explanation: keep up with = theo kịp

sub_lessons.name: Communication
sub_lessons.description: Giao tiếp - Everyday English: Asking for and giving directions. Listening và nói về cuộc sống thành phố.

sections.heading: 1. Everyday English: Asking for and giving directions
sections.content:
Asking for directions (Hỏi đường):

  • Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to...? — Xin lỗi, bạn có thể chỉ tôi đường đến...?
  • Excuse me, where is the...? — Xin lỗi, ... ở đâu?
  • Is there a... near here? — Có ... gần đây không?
  • How far is it from here? — Cách đây bao xa?

Giving directions (Chỉ đường):

  • Go straight ahead. — Đi thẳng.
  • Turn left / right. — Rẽ trái / phải.
  • Take the first / second street on the left / right. — Rẽ ở đường thứ nhất / hai bên trái / phải.
  • It's opposite / next to / between... — Nó đối diện / bên cạnh / giữa...
  • You can take bus number... — Bạn có thể đi xe buýt số...

sections.heading: 2. Practice: Pair work
sections.content:
Situation 1: You want to find a supermarket.

You: Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the nearest supermarket?

Passerby: Sure. Go straight ahead, then turn left. It's opposite the park.

Situation 2: You want to know how far the bus station is.

You: How far is it from here to the bus station?

Passerby: It's about one kilometre. You can walk there in ten minutes.

Situation 3: You want to ask about the metro.

You: Is there a metro station near here?

Passerby: Yes, the nearest one is on Nguyen Hue Street, about 500 metres from here.

sections.heading: 3. Listening: City life
sections.content:
Listen to a conversation about city life and complete the notes.

Listening passage:

Mai moved to Ha Noi two years ago. At first, she found city life very different from her hometown. The streets were always busy and crowded. However, she soon discovered the advantages of city life. The public transport system is excellent. She can easily get around by bus or metro. There are also many parks where she can relax. Mai says the more she lives in Ha Noi, the more she loves it.

Comprehension questions:

  1. How long has Mai lived in Ha Noi? → Two years.
  2. What did she find about the streets? → They were always busy and crowded.
  3. What is excellent in Ha Noi? → The public transport system.
  4. How can Mai get around? → By bus or metro.
  5. What does Mai say about living in Ha Noi? → The more she lives in Ha Noi, the more she loves it.

sections.heading: 4. Speaking: City problems and solutions
sections.content:
Work in pairs. Talk about city problems and suggest solutions.

ProblemSolution
Traffic jamsUse public transport, ride bicycles
Air pollutionPlant more trees, reduce factory emissions
Noise pollutionLimit construction at night, use soundproof glass
OvercrowdingDevelop suburbs, improve infrastructure
High cost of livingControl prices, provide affordable housing

Sample:

A: What do you think is the biggest problem in our city?

B: I think traffic jams are the biggest problem. The more vehicles there are, the worse the traffic becomes.

A: What should we do?

B: We should use public transport more. The more people use buses and metros, the fewer traffic jams there will be.

exercises.question: Which is the correct way to ask for directions politely?
options: Tell me where the station is!
Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the station?
exercises.explanation: Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to...? là cách hỏi đường lịch sự.

exercises.question: How long has Mai lived in Ha Noi?
exercises.explanation: Mai moved to Ha Noi two years ago.

exercises.question: What does Mai say about living in Ha Noi?
options: The more she lives there, the more she loves it.
exercises.explanation: Mai says 'The more she lives in Ha Noi, the more she loves it.'

exercises.question: Which is a good solution for traffic jams?
exercises.explanation: Using public transport helps reduce traffic jams.

sub_lessons.name: Skills 1
sub_lessons.description: Kỹ năng 1 - Đọc hiểu về các vấn đề đô thị và giải pháp. Nói về vấn đề và giải pháp cho thành phố.

sections.heading: 1. Reading: City problems and solutions
sections.content:
Pre-reading: Look at the pictures. What city problems can you see?

Read the passage about city problems and their solutions.

CITY PROBLEMS AND SOLUTIONS

Big cities around the world face many problems. One of the most serious is traffic congestion. Every day, millions of people travel to work, causing long traffic jams. This wastes time and increases air pollution.

To solve this, many cities have improved their public transport systems. London has a famous underground train system called the Tube. Tokyo has one of the most efficient train networks in the world. People are encouraged to leave their cars at home and use public transport. The more people use public transport, the less pollution there is.

Another problem is waste management. Big cities produce thousands of tons of rubbish every day. If waste is not handled properly, it causes pollution and health problems. Many cities have started recycling programmes. In San Francisco, residents sort their rubbish into different bins for recycling, composting, and landfill. The city has successfully reduced waste by 80%.

Noise pollution is also a concern. Construction, traffic, and industrial activities create constant noise. Cities like Vienna have built noise barriers along highways and planted trees to absorb sound.

sections.heading: 2. Comprehension Questions
sections.content:
1. What is one of the most serious problems in big cities? → Traffic congestion / traffic jams.
  2. What public transport system does London have? → The Tube (underground train system).
  3. What has San Francisco done to reduce waste? → Started recycling programmes where residents sort rubbish into different bins.
  4. How much waste has San Francisco reduced? → 80%.
  5. How have cities like Vienna reduced noise pollution? → Built noise barriers along highways and planted trees to absorb sound.
  6. What is the benefit of using public transport? → The more people use public transport, the less pollution there is.

sections.heading: 3. Speaking: Discuss city problems and solutions
sections.content:
Work in groups. Choose a city problem and discuss solutions.

Structure:

  • Problem: Identify the problem and explain why it's serious
  • Cause: What causes this problem
  • Solution: Suggest 2-3 solutions
  • Result: What will happen if we apply these solutions

Useful language:

  • The more... the more...
  • One solution is to...
  • We should / could / need to...
  • This will help to...
  • If we..., then...

Sample:

Our group chose traffic congestion. The more people own cars, the worse traffic becomes. One solution is to improve public transport. We should build more metro lines and bus routes. The more convenient public transport is, the fewer people will drive cars. Another solution is to encourage cycling by building more bike lanes. If we do these things, our city will have less pollution and fewer traffic jams.

exercises.question: What is one of the most serious problems in big cities according to the passage?
exercises.explanation: One of the most serious problems in big cities is traffic congestion.

exercises.question: What is London's underground train system called?
exercises.explanation: London has a famous underground train system called the Tube.

exercises.question: How much waste has San Francisco reduced through recycling?
exercises.explanation: San Francisco has successfully reduced waste by 80%.

exercises.question: What has Vienna done to reduce noise pollution?
options: Built noise barriers and planted trees
exercises.explanation: Vienna built noise barriers along highways and planted trees to absorb sound.

exercises.question: According to the passage, what happens if more people use public transport?
exercises.explanation: The more people use public transport, the less pollution there is.

sub_lessons.name: Skills 2
sub_lessons.description: Kỹ năng 2 - Nghe về cuộc sống thành phố và viết đoạn văn về city life.

sections.heading: 1. Listening: City life
sections.content:
Pre-listening: What do you know about life in a big city? What are the good and bad things?

Listen to a passage about a person's experience living in a big city.

Listening passage:

My name is Lan. I moved to Ho Chi Minh City three years ago for university. At first, I was overwhelmed by the fast pace of life. The streets were always crowded and noisy. However, I quickly adapted. Now I really enjoy living here. The city has excellent facilities: modern shopping malls, beautiful parks, and great restaurants. The public transport is convenient, so it's easy to get around. I have made many friends and I love the energy of the city. The more time I spend here, the more I appreciate city life. Living in a big city has taught me to be independent and confident. I believe that the bigger the city is, the more opportunities it offers.

Comprehension questions:

  1. How long has Lan lived in Ho Chi Minh City? → Three years.
  2. Why was Lan overwhelmed at first? → By the fast pace of life.
  3. What does Lan enjoy about the city now? → Excellent facilities, modern shopping malls, parks, restaurants, convenient public transport.
  4. What has city life taught Lan? → To be independent and confident.
  5. What does Lan believe about big cities? → The bigger the city is, the more opportunities it offers.

sections.heading: 2. Writing: A paragraph about city life
sections.content:
Structure:

PartContent
Topic sentenceIntroduce city life in general or a specific city
BodyDescribe advantages (facilities, transport, opportunities)
BodyDescribe disadvantages (crowded, noisy, expensive)
Concluding sentenceYour opinion about city life

Useful language:

  • Advantages: convenient, modern, exciting, many opportunities, good facilities, excellent public transport
  • Disadvantages: crowded, noisy, polluted, expensive, stressful, traffic jams
  • Comparatives: The more... the more...; bigger than, more expensive than, better than

Sample paragraph:

Life in a big city has both advantages and disadvantages. On the one hand, the city offers many opportunities for education and employment. There are modern facilities such as shopping malls, hospitals, and sports centres. The public transport system makes it easy to get around. On the other hand, the city is often crowded and noisy. The more people move to the city, the more crowded it becomes. Traffic jams are a daily problem. In my opinion, despite the challenges, city life is exciting and worth experiencing.

exercises.question: How long has Lan lived in Ho Chi Minh City?
exercises.explanation: Lan moved to Ho Chi Minh City three years ago.

exercises.question: What was Lan overwhelmed by at first?
exercises.explanation: Lan was overwhelmed by the fast pace of life.

exercises.question: What has city life taught Lan?
exercises.explanation: Living in a big city has taught Lan to be independent and confident.

exercises.question: What does Lan believe about big cities?
options: The bigger the city, the more dangerous it is.
The bigger the city, the more opportunities it offers.
The bigger the city, the more polluted it is.
The bigger the city, the more lonely people feel.
exercises.explanation: Lan believes the bigger the city is, the more opportunities it offers.

exercises.question: Write a paragraph (80-100 words) about city life. Include both advantages and disadvantages. Use at least one double comparative (the more... the more...).
exercises.sample_answer: City life has both good and bad sides. The city offers many job opportunities and modern facilities like shopping malls, parks, and hospitals. Public transport is convenient, so it is easy to get around. However, the city is also crowded and noisy. The more people move to the city, the more traffic jams there are. Air pollution is also a serious problem. Despite these disadvantages, I enjoy city life because it is exciting and full of opportunities.
exercises.explanation: Học sinh viết đoạn văn về city life, nêu ưu và nhược điểm, sử dụng so sánh kép.

sub_lessons.name: Looking Back
sub_lessons.description: Ôn tập từ vựng và ngữ pháp toàn bộ Unit 2: City Life.

sections.heading: 1. Vocabulary Review
sections.content:
City features: skyscraper, shopping mall, public transport, metro, infrastructure, entertainment

City problems: traffic jam, air pollution, noise pollution, overcrowding, high cost of living, crime

City advantages: convenient, modern, job opportunities, good education, healthcare, entertainment

sections.heading: 2. Grammar Review
sections.content:
Double Comparatives (So sánh kép):

  • The + adj-er + S + V, the + adj-er + S + V (tính từ ngắn)
  • The more + adj + S + V, the more + adj + S + V (tính từ dài)
  • The more + N + S + V, the more + N + S + V (danh từ)

Phrasal verbs: get around, grow up, come back, go on, take off, deal with, run out of, keep up with, put on, take over

exercises.question: The more carefully you drive, the ______ accidents you will cause.
exercises.explanation: The more carefully + the fewer accidents (cẩn thận hơn → ít tai nạn hơn).

exercises.question: The older I get, the ______ I become.
exercises.explanation: old → older; wise → wiser (tính từ ngắn: 1 âm tiết)

exercises.question: She had to ______ many difficulties when she moved to the city.
exercises.explanation: deal with = giải quyết, đối phó

exercises.question: The train ______ at 6 a.m. from Ha Noi station.
exercises.explanation: take off = cất cánh, khởi hành (tàu/xe)

exercises.question: They decided to ______ a new business in the city centre.
exercises.explanation: set up = thành lập

exercises.question: A ______ is a very tall building in a city.
exercises.explanation: skyscraper = nhà chọc trời

exercises.question: ______ means the system of buses, trains, etc. for people to travel.
exercises.explanation: Public transport = phương tiện giao thông công cộng

exercises.question: The ______ I learn English, the more I enjoy it.
exercises.explanation: The more I learn English = càng học tiếng Anh nhiều

exercises.question: We must ______ this problem before it gets worse.
exercises.explanation: deal with = giải quyết

exercises.question: It's not easy to ______ the rapid changes in technology.
exercises.explanation: keep up with = theo kịp

sub_lessons.name: Project
sub_lessons.description: Dự án: Tìm hiểu và thuyết trình về các vấn đề của thành phố và đề xuất giải pháp.

sections.heading: Project Instructions
sections.content:
Work in groups. Choose a city problem, research it, and present your findings and solutions.

Step 1: Choose a problem

  • Traffic congestion (kẹt xe)
  • Air pollution (ô nhiễm không khí)
  • Noise pollution (ô nhiễm tiếng ồn)
  • Overcrowding (quá đông đúc)
  • Waste management (quản lý rác thải)
  • High cost of living (chi phí sinh hoạt cao)

Step 2: Prepare your presentation

PartContent
IntroductionIntroduce the problem and which city you're talking about
DescriptionDescribe the problem: how serious it is, examples
CausesWhat causes this problem
SolutionsSuggest 2-3 practical solutions
ConclusionSummarise and call for action

sections.heading: Sample Project: Traffic congestion in Ha Noi
sections.content:
Traffic Congestion in Ha Noi

Today our group will talk about traffic congestion in Ha Noi. This is a serious problem that affects millions of people every day.

During rush hours, many streets are blocked with vehicles. People waste hours sitting in traffic. The more vehicles there are, the worse the congestion becomes. This also causes air pollution because cars and motorbikes release harmful gases.

The main cause is the rapid increase in the number of private vehicles. More people buy motorbikes and cars, but the roads haven't expanded enough.

There are several solutions. First, the government should improve public transport by building more metro lines and bus routes. Second, people should be encouraged to use bicycles for short distances. Third, we need better traffic management with smart traffic lights.

In conclusion, traffic congestion is a big challenge for Ha Noi. But if we work together, we can make our city a better place to live. The more we act now, the better our future will be. Thank you for listening.

exercises.question: Write a short proposal (80-100 words) about a city problem and suggest solutions. Use at least one double comparative (the more... the more...).
exercises.sample_answer: Our group chose air pollution in Ho Chi Minh City. The more factories and vehicles there are, the more polluted the air becomes. This causes health problems for residents. To solve this, we should plant more trees in the city. Trees absorb CO2 and release oxygen. The government should also encourage people to use electric vehicles instead of petrol cars. Factories must install air filters. If we do these things, the air quality will improve. Everyone should take action to protect our environment.
exercises.explanation: Học sinh viết đề xuất về vấn đề thành phố và giải pháp, sử dụng so sánh kép.
